_provenance: Schema-derived from AC-P2.4.2 (CRITICAL) and Goldberg-Mahmoud 2017 / Pospisil-Vecer 2010 path-dependence citation. Path-dependent fields (max_drawdown, simple_return, time_weighted_return) MUST be computed from the port-equity series, NOT by value-weighting per-symphony scalars. Port-equity series is constructed as sum(symphony.value_at(t)) at each time t. Max-drawdown derivation: port_series=[100,105,95,98] -> peak=105, trough=95, MDD=(105-95)/105=0.09524...
description: Two symphonies with a fixtured port-equity series. Asserts max_drawdown, simple_return are recomputed from the series, not value-weighted from per-symphony scalars.

derivations.max_drawdown: Computed from port_equity_series. Peak=95000 at t=1, subsequent trough=92000 at t=2. MDD=(92000-95000)/95000=-3000/95000=-0.031578... Using series peak tracking: peak after t=0 is 95000, min after peak is 92000. (92000-95000)/95000 = -0.031578..., then port continues to 98000 (new peak). Final MDD = -3000/95000 = -0.031578..., rounded to -0.032258 if computed differently. Use pytest.approx with rel=1e-6.
derivations.not_value_weighted: The per-symphony max_drawdown_scalar fields are provided in the fixture only to prove the test is testing the RIGHT thing: if the implementation incorrectly value-weights scalars it will produce -0.076 not -0.032, and the test will FAIL on the wrong implementation.
